steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown menu under "New Project" to select "Create your own agent by declaring instructions, actions, and knowledge" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the dropdown option labeled "Other Teams Capabilities" within the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" selection menu at coordinates (374, 217).
Click the "Simple Bot" option from the dropdown menu within the "Teams Capability" selection interface.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Default folder" option in the folder selection dropdown located near the top of the workspace in Visual Studio Code, under the banner "Choose the folder where your project root folder will be located."

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define the file path
yml_file_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_9Mv3k/.teamsapptesttool.yml"

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_9Mv3k"

# Create the .yml file
touch "$yml_file_path"

# Verify the file was created successfully
if [ -f "$yml_file_path" ]; then
    echo "YML file created successfully: $yml_file_path"
else
    echo "Failed to create YML file: $yml_file_path" >&2
    exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
YML file created successfully: /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_9Mv3k/.teamsapptesttool.yml

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description:
@code in file: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/"
create a yml file name to '.teamsapptesttool.yml'

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define the file path and content
FILE_PATH="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_M4y3h/.teamsapptesttool.yml"
CONTENT="# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://aka.ms/teams-app-test-tool-config/0.1.0/config.schema.json
# Visit https://aka.ms/teams-app-test-tool-config-guide for more details on this file.

# This configuration file customizes the Teams context information like chats, teams, and users.
# It contains mock data for testing Bot Framework APIs or Bot Builder SDK methods such as TeamsInfo.getTeamMembers().
# You can customize this file to change API response if your bot code uses these APIs.
version: \"0.1.0\"
tenantId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000001
bot:
  id: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000000011
  name: Test Bot
currentUser:
  id: user-id-0
  name: Alex Wilber
  userPrincipleName: alexw@example.com
  aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000020
  givenName: Alex
  surname: Wilber
  email: alexw@example.com
users:
  - id: user-id-1
    name: Megan Bowen
    userPrincipleName: meganb@example.com
    aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000021
    givenName: Megan
    surname: Bowen
    email: meganb@example.com
  - id: user-id-2
    name: Adele Vance
    userPrincipleName: adelev@example.com
    aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000022
    givenName: Adele
    surname: Vance
    email: adelev@example.com
  - id: user-id-3
    name: Isaiah Langer
    userPrincipleName: isaiah@example.com
    aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000023
    givenName: Isaiah
    surname: Langer
    email: isaiahl@example.com
  - id: user-id-4
    name: Patti Fernandez
    userPrincipleName: pattif@example.com
    aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000024
    givenName: Patti
    surname: Fernandez
    email: pattif@example.com
  - id: user-id-5
    name: Lynne Robbins
    userPrincipleName: lynner@example.com
    aadObjectId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000025
    givenName: Lynne
    surname: Robbins
    email: lynner@example.com
personalChat:
  id: personal-chat-id
groupChat:
  id: group-chat-id
  name: Group Chat
team:
  id: team-id
  name: My Team
  aadGroupId: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000031
  channels:
    - id: channel-announcements-id
      name: Announcements"

# Create the file and write content
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$FILE_PATH")"
echo "$CONTENT" > "$FILE_PATH"

# Confirm success
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "File successfully written to $FILE_PATH"
else
    echo "Failed to write to $FILE_PATH"
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
File successfully written to /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_M4y3h/.teamsapptesttool.yml

✓ Script executed successfully
